name: Founder Comic Strip
description: Campaign story section rendered as bold comic/manual panels for maker origin, product problem, prototype breakthrough, and launch ask.

story-panels.description: Four to six comic-style story panels with captions, maker photos or illustrations, and a clear campaign ask.

slots.story-panel: Repeating panel with image/diagram, label, title, and short caption.
slots.campaign-ask: What support enables next, with CTA.

manual-origin.description: Instruction-manual origin story with numbered product development steps and maker credibility notes.

slots.maker-proof: Credentials, prototype count, customer quotes, or build progress.

visual_brief: A founder story told through bold panels rather than a wall of prose. Each panel should have a clear scene: problem, prototype, user signal, and what backing unlocks. Use photos, product sketches, UI screenshots, or simple diagrams where possible. The style should be playful but honest; campaign trust matters.

responsive.mobile: Single-column comic strip. Keep captions short and readable.
responsive.tablet: Two-column panel grid with maker note spanning full width.
responsive.desktop: Editorial comic strip or staggered grid. Avoid nested panel groups.

layout_hints.honesty: Do not overclaim. Include current product state and what funding/support enables.
layout_hints.media: Prefer actual maker/product evidence over decorative illustration.

micro.panel-hover: panel shadow shifts hard by 2px; no soft elevation
micro.caption-reveal: caption line appears with short snap-in

transitions.story-stagger: Panels enter one by one as the reader scrolls.

keyboard: Interactive story panels should expose detail buttons when expandable.
Panel order must match visual reading order.
